highlights.summary: AllNighters crushed the Wolfpack 11-4 at Marrs Park on Monday in a dominant home performance. The Nighters jumped out early with two runs in the first inning aided by Wolfpack walks, and never looked back. Joe Zametjis led the offense going 3-for-4 with a double and driving in two, while Eric (E) Moody went 2-for-2. Darren (Dermbo) McDermott, Jason Farrell, and Mike Russo each chipped in with key hits. A positive sign on the night was very few runners left on base and most outs were hard-hit balls. Mike (The OG) Russo was dominant on the mound again, going all 7 innings allowing just 4 runs on 10 hits. Even a long bomb by the Wolfpack in the 7th did not rattle Russo or the Nighters. The defense made some clutch catches in the middle innings to truly deliver a heart punch to the Pack. Next up - the newly stacked Paddy Kelleys who appear to have doubled their payroll in one last push for glory. The Nighters will need to bring the juice!

AllNighters could not hold onto the lead against Pocket 9s on Wednesday at Raddin Park and fell 5-4 despite leading by three going into the bottom of the 5th. Robert (The Godfather) Barrows was the offensive highlight for AllNighters going 3 for 3 with three line shots to the right side, while Patrick Hosman, Brendan (Btro) Trohon, and Ryan Overberg each added two hits. A single by Overberg put AllNighters on the board in the top of the second and then the crew rallied in the 4th. AllNighters collected 14 hits in the game. Unfortunately they left 11 men on base, hit into two double plays, and stranded the bases loaded in the top of the 7th. Barrows, Overberg, and Joe Zametjis each drove in one run. Pocket 9s finished things off with a walk-off single RBI in the bottom of the 7th. If the Allnighters look to repeat this year, they better get their bats a little warmer.

AllNighters defeated Franchise 9-5 on Monday. AllNighters jumped out to the lead in the bottom of the first inning after Brendan (Btro) Trohon singled scoring one run, Joe Zametjis homered scoring three runs, and Darren (Dermbo) McDermott singled scoring one run. A single by Brendan (Bmac) McCarthy, a single by Trohon, and a double by Jason Farrell helped AllNighters extend their early lead in the second. Mike (The OG) Russo earned the win for AllNighters. The starting pitcher gave up seven hits and five runs over seven innings striking out none and walking none. AllNighters collected 14 hits in the game. Patrick Hosman, McCarthy, Eric (E) Moody, and Trohon each collected two hits. Zametjis led AllNighters with three runs batted in going 1-for-3 on the day.
